summary: The team conducted a post-incident review of a cascading failure in their event ingestion pipeline that caused six hours of zero threat monitoring visibility for customers. Mike Romano explained that an out-of-memory error on a primary ingestion node triggered a cascade due to the absence of a circuit breaker and inadequate health checks that couldn't distinguish between liveness and readiness. The group identified three root technical causes: a single point of failure in ingestion, no circuit breaker pattern, and insufficient health checking. The team also acknowledged that the underlying single-point-of-failure risk had been flagged in Q3 of the prior year but was deprioritized in favor of feature work, and agreed the post-mortem should reflect that honestly.
actionItems: Mike Romano: Write a proper scope document today for circuit breaker implementation and redundant ingestion node architecture
Ananya Sharma and Tyler Washington: Investigate and implement health check improvements, specifically adding readiness checks
Nina Kowalski: Scope out a chaos testing framework for the ingestion pipeline, coordinating with Tyler Washington
Sofia Petrov: Circulate the post-mortem draft by end of day tomorrow, including transparent account of known-risk deprioritization

keyMoments.text: Sofia expresses frustration that a known single point of failure was never prioritized, resulting in six hours of zero customer threat monitoring visibility
keyMoments.speaker: Sofia Petrov

keyMoments.text: Tyler identifies that the health check was a liveness check rather than a readiness check, making it effectively useless in detecting the stuck node
keyMoments.speaker: Tyler Washington

keyMoments.text: Nina requests the two-week circuit breaker estimate in writing, and Mike agrees to produce a formal scope document
keyMoments.speaker: Nina Kowalski

keyMoments.text: Ananya pushes for full transparency in the post-mortem about the known risk being deprioritized, and Sofia agrees the decision-making chain must be documented honestly
keyMoments.speaker: Ananya Sharma
